hero_product_name: the AnyWell YS16-1 PLUS electric wheelchair from the white-background reference photos
campaign_goal: Create two universal 9:16 short ads for the AnyWell YS16-1 PLUS, inspired by the emotional idea of getting back outdoors near home without recreating any real customer story.
target_audience: American seniors who need mobility support, their spouses, and adult children buying for loved ones.
core_selling_points:
- smooth 500W dual motors
- wide 22-inch seat
- supportive outdoor everyday mobility
- calm stable starts near home
use_scenarios:
- home threshold to yard path
- finished ramp to level ground
- quiet sidewalk or lane near home
visual_style: Realistic, warm, portrait-first American lifestyle advertising with clean route continuity and distinct scene progression.
style_tone: warm, calm, dignified, persuasive, practical, never clinical
consistency_anchor: Match the real YS16-1 PLUS wheelchair from the white-background product photos while keeping it only in normal open riding position. Preserve the right-side joystick, wide seat feel, frame, footrest, wheel layout, and real product silhouette. Keep the rear/top-back area compact and proportional to the real chair: no extra rods, poles, antenna-like parts, cane-like extensions, or exaggerated push bars behind the backrest. In every shot, keep the rider and wheelchair proportion close to c1 and c2: the rider naturally fills the broad seat, the chair reads substantial and premium, and neither subject looks oversized or undersized relative to the other.
additional_info: Hard Meta ad rules: use a white-haired American senior husband age 60+ with a matching wife, keep the same couple across connected scenes, and keep actions simple and safe. Use Jean's uploaded clip only as emotional direction for freedom outdoors near home. Do not recreate a real person, a real house, an unfinished ramp, the exact woods layout, or testimonial behavior. Do not place any readable ad copy, price tags, UI overlays, or gibberish text inside the generated footage itself. Keep each shot clean and reserve negative space only for post-added captions or offer overlays. Spoken lines should be short, natural American English sentences that Veo can deliver cleanly.
music: Warm restrained cinematic underscore with light piano and a subtle modern pulse.

concepts.title: From Doorway to Garden
concepts.core_theme: A calm backyard route that clearly progresses away from the house and into open air.
concepts.main_theme: Fresh air should still feel close.
concepts.hook: Fresh air should still feel close.
concepts.cta: See how AnyWell helps bring the outdoors back within reach.
concepts.visual_style: Soft morning light, backyard route, wife nearby, emotional but grounded and believable.
concepts.continuity_rider_anchor: Keep the same heavy-set white-haired American senior husband age 60+ and the same wife across all three shots. He should visibly fill the wide seat naturally, with chair scale and framing similar to c1 and c2.
concepts.use_scenarios:
- back doorway or covered patio threshold
- narrow garden path beside the home
- quiet sidewalk just beyond the garden gate
concepts.additional_info: This concept should feel like one continuous outing. Scene one must clearly show the home threshold, scene two must clearly feel deeper in the garden with greenery close to camera, and scene three must clearly open into a quieter sidewalk beyond the yard. Keep screen direction left to right across all three shots.

shots.scene_description: A heavy-set white-haired American senior husband in his 60s calmly drives the YS16-1 PLUS from a bright back doorway or covered patio threshold into the yard while his wife opens space for him and follows with a relieved smile. This must read as the first step of one continuous route: the doorframe, patio edge, and first movement away from the house should be clearly visible. Frame like c1 and c2 with the rider and chair large in portrait, the wide seat clearly supporting his body, and the wheelchair reading full-sized and premium. Keep the motion moving left to right and leave clean upper-corner negative space, but keep the generated footage itself free of in-scene ad text or UI.
shots.camera_movement: smooth front-side push that begins nearly still at the threshold and eases outward into the yard, keeping left-to-right motion
shots.lighting: soft morning daylight with warm highlights and natural depth
shots.composition_and_set_dressing: portrait medium-wide composition, rider and chair large in frame, visible back door or patio threshold, generic North American home details only, no exact source-house cues
shots.transition_anchor: end with the chair rolling fully off the patio and onto the garden walk, still moving left to right
shots.voiceover_en: Fresh air should still feel close.
shots.key_message: The outdoors can begin right at home.
shots.sfx: soft wheel roll, birds, and light morning ambience

shots.scene_description: The same husband now glides farther from the house on a narrow garden path bordered by shrubs, flowers, or lawn edges, with his right hand clearly on the joystick while his wife walks one step behind him. This must feel like the middle section of the same route, not a repeated doorway shot: greenery should sit closer to camera, the house should feel farther back, and the path should feel more enclosed than scene one. Keep c1 and c2 proportion logic so the rider visibly fills the 22-inch seat naturally and the chair remains large and balanced in frame. Maintain left-to-right screen direction and keep the generated footage itself free of in-scene ad text or UI.
shots.camera_movement: steady joystick-side front-profile tracking shot with subtle forward drift and tight garden parallax
shots.lighting: clean daylight filtered through trees with premium but realistic contrast
shots.composition_and_set_dressing: portrait medium shot with rider and chair taking most of the frame height, shrubs or flowers close to camera, spouse softly visible, the home only distant or partial
shots.transition_anchor: end with the chair approaching a small gate opening or sidewalk break at the edge of the yard, still moving left to right
shots.voiceover_en: A smooth ride makes the garden feel open again.
shots.key_message: Comfort and control are visible in motion.
shots.sfx: smooth motor ambience, leaves, and light outdoor air

shots.scene_description: The same couple emerge onto a quiet residential sidewalk just beyond the garden gate, creating a clear final step from home to open air. This must read as a new destination: more sky, more open space, less dense greenery, and a calmer wider feeling than the previous shot. He slows into a warm lifestyle hero frame while seated naturally in the chair and she walks beside him with visible relief. Keep soft background separation, preserve left-to-right route logic, and reserve clean negative space for post-added captions while the generated footage itself stays free of in-scene ad text or UI.
shots.camera_movement: gentle moving settle from the sidewalk approach into a confident portrait hero frame
shots.lighting: warm flattering daylight with a polished but realistic finish
shots.composition_and_set_dressing: portrait hero composition on a quiet sidewalk or lane, rider and chair prominent, open sky or open space visible, fewer nearby plants than scene two
shots.transition_anchor: end on a calm confident hold for the close
shots.voiceover_en: Freedom should feel steady, simple, and safe.
shots.key_message: The route closes on confidence and dignity.
shots.sfx: music lift and soft outdoor ambience

concepts.title: A Gentle Ramp Out
concepts.core_theme: A finished ramp creates a believable, reassuring route from home to level ground and then into the evening.
concepts.main_theme: The first few feet should feel calm.
concepts.hook: The first few feet should feel calm.
concepts.cta: Watch how a smoother start changes the whole outing.
concepts.visual_style: Late-afternoon light, finished ramp, clean home exterior, soft evening close.
concepts.continuity_rider_anchor: Keep the same heavy-set white-haired American senior husband and the same wife across all three shots. Keep the rider-chair scale substantial and natural like c1 and c2.
concepts.use_scenarios:
- front porch or side entry threshold
- finished wooden or composite ramp
- quiet driveway lane or sidewalk at golden hour
concepts.additional_info: This concept must show a real route progression with obvious visual differences: scene one shows the threshold before the slope, scene two shows the ramp and controlled descent, scene three shows level ground with warmer evening light. Do not recreate Jean's unfinished ramp. Keep screen direction consistent from left to right.

shots.scene_description: A heavy-set white-haired American senior husband starts moving from a clean porch threshold or side entry toward a finished ramp in the YS16-1 PLUS while his wife watches with a warm relieved expression. This must clearly read as the moment before the slope: show the doorway, landing, and ramp entrance in the same frame. Use c1 and c2 proportion cues so the broad supportive seat, natural heavy-set posture, and full-sized chair all read clearly in portrait. Keep left-to-right route motion and leave clean top-corner negative space, but keep the generated footage itself free of in-scene ad text or UI.
shots.camera_movement: slow front-side move that follows the first start toward the ramp entrance, keeping the route moving left to right
shots.lighting: bright late-afternoon daylight with soft warm reflections from the ramp and siding
shots.composition_and_set_dressing: portrait medium-wide threshold and ramp-entry frame, rider and chair prominent, little empty headroom, generic home exterior only
shots.transition_anchor: end as the chair reaches the start of the finished ramp and begins the descent, still moving left to right
shots.voiceover_en: The first few feet should feel calm.
shots.sfx: soft porch ambience and subtle wheel start

shots.scene_description: The same husband travels down the finished ramp and reaches level ground with his right hand using a natural thumb-and-index-finger precision pinch on the joystick knob while his wife walks nearby with hands free. This must clearly feel like the middle proof shot, not another threshold: the ramp slope should be readable, the controlled descent should be visible, and level ground should open up ahead of him. Keep the rider-chair ratio believable like c1 and c2 with fuller body naturally seated in the wide chair, strong wheel size, and no toy-like scale. Maintain left-to-right screen direction and keep the generated footage itself free of in-scene ad text or UI.
shots.camera_movement: smooth front-quarter arc that follows the controlled ramp descent and settles as the chair reaches level ground
shots.lighting: clear daylight with mild highlights and realistic shadows
shots.composition_and_set_dressing: portrait medium shot, finished ramp visible, chair and rider take most of the frame, open driveway or lane visible ahead
shots.transition_anchor: end with the chair fully on level ground and naturally continuing forward toward the lane
shots.voiceover_en: Steady power makes every start easier to trust.
shots.key_message: Control and stability are the proof.
shots.sfx: quiet wheel roll, light breeze, and yard ambience

shots.scene_description: The same couple continue onto a quiet driveway lane or sidewalk in warmer early-evening light, creating a clear final beat after the ramp. This must read as the arrival scene: the slope is behind them, the space feels more open, and the mood is softer and more relaxed than the prior shot. Keep the husband seated naturally and proportionately in the chair with strong c1 and c2 scale cues, and reserve clean negative space for post-added captions while the generated footage itself stays free of in-scene ad text or UI.
shots.camera_movement: slow settle into a warm hero frame after a short forward glide on level ground
shots.lighting: gentle golden-hour light with a polished but realistic finish
shots.composition_and_set_dressing: portrait close-medium frame on quiet level ground, chair still readable, ramp no longer dominant, soft open space for the close
shots.transition_anchor: end on a still frame for brand lockup or CTA
shots.voiceover_en: A good day can begin right outside.
shots.key_message: The close lands on ease, dignity, and emotional relief.
shots.sfx: music rise and gentle evening ambience
